mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice risk assets.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.5% fail to outperform baseline by >1.5% (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market demand, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS changes (Rank IC < 0.05) over 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving consumer confidence and business investment, creating positive momentum in equity markets over 20 trading days through demand-side catalysts.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns underperform baseline by >2% when HOUST exceeds 1300k (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals contraction in manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant underperformance when INDPRO falls below 105.0 (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal accommodative policy and deflationary risks, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through yield curve inversion effects.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% when both CPIAUCSL < 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong demand-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sector rotation effects.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns underperform baseline by >1% when RSAFS > 490k and UNRATE < 4.0% (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent labor market tightness (UNRATE < 4.0%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through central bank tightening expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns exceed 2% when UNRATE < 4.0% and PAYEMS > 150k (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production (INDPRO < 106.0) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) indicates stagflation risks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through dual demand-supply shocks.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% when INDPRO < 106.0 and FEDFUNDS > 2.0% (t-test p > 0.1).
